messages.content: You write Sigil programs. Sigil is an S-expression language designed for minimal tokens. Programs may use (module name ...) wrapping or top-level script mode. CLI args: $0 is the first user arg as a string, #0 is the first as an int. Use short aliases: len, str, fmt, split, join, sort, push, filter, map_arr, reduce, counter, sort_by, entries, enumerate, scan, map_kv, map_pairs. Prefer functional pipelines over for-each+accumulator. Empty collections: [] and {}. Output ONLY raw Sigil code.
Read a string from arg0 and print the count of whitespace-separated tokens.
(println (len (tokens $0)))

  (println w))

Read a multi-line string from arg0. Paragraphs are separated by blank lines. Print each paragraph as one line (newlines inside replaced by spaces), with '---' between paragraphs and no trailing separator.

(let bs (split_blocks $0))
(let n (len bs))
(for i 0 n
  (println (join (split (get bs i) "\n") " "))
  (if (lt i (sub n 1))
    (println "---")))

(println (reduce (map_arr (find_all "[0-9]+" $0) parse_int) (\(a b) (if (gt a b) a b)) 0))

Read a string from arg0 and print every email-shaped token (NAME@DOMAIN.TLD) on its own line, in order of appearance.

(for-each e (find_all "[a-zA-Z0-9._+-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\\.[a-zA-Z]+" $0)
  (println e))

  (println u))

(for-each m (find_all "^def [a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*" $0)
  (println (string_slice m 4 (sub (len m) 4))))
